channel.title: Home on Lin Wang (Paderborn University)
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Lin Wang (Paderborn University)

item.title: Rules
item.description: Time Management Your time is precious and you should be cautious about how you spend it. As Hamming pointed out in his famous talk “You and Your Research”, opportunities favor minds that are well prepared. You should always ask yourself: Am I well prepared for something? If not, how far are your from that? What are required for you to reach that? Answers to these questions will motivate you to spend your time more wisely.

item.title: Advanced Computer Networks (X_405082) 2020
item.description: X_405082 is a granduate course in computer networks. In this course we will discuss advanced topics in computer networking. The context will span broadly from the network core, across the Internet, all the way to the network edge. The objectives of this course are: To learn about the recent/current developments in computer networks To understand and reason about the design of these technologies To be able to build complex networked systems and evaluate them Course Information Lecturer: Lin Wang

item.title: Advanced Computer Networks (X_405082) 2021
item.description: In this course we will discuss advanced topics in computer networking. We will cover concepts and designs for modern network systems such as data center networks, software-defined networks, programmable networks, and cutting-edge networking research topics (such as network automation, in-network computing, and machine learning for networking). The objectives of this course are: to learn about the recent/current developments in computer networks to understand and reason about the design of these technologies to be able to build complex networked systems and evaluate them The study guide of the course can be found here.

item.title: Advanced Computer Networks (X_405082) 2022
item.description: In this course we will discuss advanced topics in computer networking. We will cover concepts and designs for modern network systems such as data center networking, software-defined networking, programmable networks, and cutting-edge networking research topics (such as programmable switch architectures and in-network computing). The objectives of this course are: Gain the knowledge of current research topics in computer networking Understand and reason about the design of new networking technologies Build complex networked systems and evaluate them More can be found on the study guide webpage.

item.title: News
item.description: 2023 12.08.2023: Paper on routing with intermittency accepted to IEEE IPCCC. 29.04.2023: I will serve on the PC for ACM SoCC 2023 and IEEE INFOCOM 2024. 13.03.2023: I will serve on the PC for ACM/IEEE SEC 2023. 13.01.2023: I will serve on the PC for ACM ApPLIED@PODC 2023. 2022 18.12.2022: All materials for MSc course Advanced Computer Networks 2022 are released here. 13.12.2022: Received a donation of two Netberg Tofino switches from Intel.

item.title: Publications
item.description: Here is a selected list of my publications. Please check DBLP or similar sites for a complete list. 2024 NetCL: A Unified Programming Framework for In-Network Computing George Karlos, Henri Bal, Lin Wang IEEE/ACM SC, Atlanta, GA, 2024. (Accepted) A Little Certainty is All We Need: Discovery and Synchronization Acceleration in Battery-Free IoT Gaosheng Liu, Vinode Nigade, Henri Bal, Lin Wang ACM APNet, co-located with ACM SIGCOMM, Sydney, Australia, 2024. (Accepted)

item.title: Reading List for In-Network Computing
item.description: Switch Hardware [SIGCOMM'13] Forwarding metamorphosis: fast programmable match-action processing in hardware for SDN [link] [SIGCOMM'17] dRMT: disaggregated programmable switching [link] [ISCA'21] A RISC-V in-network accelerator for flexible high-performance low-power packet processing [link] [SIGCOMM'22] Programmable multi-dimensional table filters for line rate network functions [link] [ASPLOS'22] Taurus: a data plane architecture for per-packet ML [link] [NSDI'22] Isolation mechanisms for high-speed packet-processing pipelines [link] [NSDI'22] Runtime programmable switches [link] NICs Programming Languages and Compilers [NSDI'15] Compiling packet programs to reconfigurable switches [link] [SIGCOMM'16] Packet transactions: high-level programming for line-rate switches [link] [SIGCOMM'20] Switch code generation using program synthesis [link] [arXiv'22] High-level synthesis for packet-processing pipelines [link] Applications [SC'21] Flare: flexible in-network allreduce [link] [VLDB'21] In-network support for transaction triaging [link]

item.title: Research
item.description: I am generally interested in distributed systems and networking. In particular, my current research focuses on the following two lines. Latency-Critical Inference Serving Emerging mobile and ubiquitous applications demand real-time, reliable performance which can benefit from decentralized, close-to-user deployments in the field. For example, augmented reality (AR) applications running on mobile devices can leverage nearby compute servers to perform heavy object/action recognition tasks powered by machine learning on mobile devices or over the mobile network at the edge.

item.title: Services
item.description: The following lists some of my recent service activities for the research community. Program Committee ACM SoCC 2024 IEEE ICCD 2024 ACM/IEEE SEC 2024 IEEE/ACM IWQoS 2024 IEEE ICDCS 2024 ACM MobiHoc 2024 ACM ApPLIED@PODC 2024 IEEE INFOCOM 2024 ACM SoCC 2023 ACM/IEEE SEC 2023 ACM ApPLIED@PODC 2023 ACM MobiHoc 2023 IEEE ICDCS 2023 ACM/IFIP Middleware 2023 ACM SoCC 2022 IEEE ICDCS 2022 ACM ApPLIED@PODC 2022 ACM EMDL@MobiSys 2022 Organizing Committee ACM SIGCOMM 2023 (Web Co-Chair) ACM SIGCOMM 2022 (Web Co-Chair) IFIP Networking 2021 (Session Chair) EDL Edge Workshop 2021 (Co-Chair) ACM WEEE@e-Energy 2020 (Co-Chair) IEEE HotSALSA@INFOCOM 2019 (Co-Chair) IEEE ECN@ICCCN 2019 (Co-Chair) SFB MAKI Industry Workshop 2018 (Co-Chair) Journal Reviewer ACM Transactions on Internet Technology (TOIT) ACM Transactions on Embedded Computing Systems (TECS) IEEE/ACM Transactions on Networking (ToN) IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems (TPDS) IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications (JSAC) IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing (TMC) Funding Proposal Referee German Research Foundation (DFG) Israel Science Foundation (ISF) Hong Kong Research Grants Council

item.title: Teaching
item.description: Here is a list of courses I have taught or am currently teaching. Master Courses Advanced Networked Systems SS24 Seminar: In-Network Computing SS24 Advanced Computer Networks (X_405082) 2022 Advanced Computer Networks (X_405082) 2021 Advanced Computer Networks (X_405082) 2020 Bachelor Courses Computer Networks WS23/24 Introduction to Computer Science (X_401087) 2023 Advanced Network Programming (XB_0048) 2022 Introduction to Computer Science (X_401087) 2022 Advanced Network Programming (XB_0048) 2021 Introduction to Computer Science (X_401087) 2021 Advanced Network Programming (XB_0048) 2020 Introduction to Computer Science (X_401087) 2020 Introduction to Computer Science (X_401087) 2019

item.title: Team
item.description: I am fortunate to work with the following talented researchers. I am actively looking for new PhD students and postdocs to join my research group. Please reach out if you are interested. Current Members Vinod Nigade (Postdoc researcher, VU Amsterdam) Kamran Razavi (PhD researcher, TU Darmstadt) Gaosheng Liu (PhD researcher, VU Amsterdam) George Karlos (PhD researcher, VU Amsterdam), co-advised with Henri Bal Guilherme Henrique (PhD researcher, VU Amsterdam), co-advised with Henri Bal Past Members Vinod Nigade (PhD, VU Amsterdam), co-advised with Henri Bal Marcel Blöcher (PhD, TU Darmstadt), co-advised with Patrick Eugster Shutong Chen (visiting PhD researcher) Florian Gerlinghoff (student assistant) Master Theses Supervised Ramon Winder (VU Amsterdam) [thesis] Marios Kanakis (VU Amsterdam): [thesis] Bart de Haan (VU Amsterdam) [thesis] Rishikumar Radhakrishnan (VU Amsterdam) [thesis] Menno Vermeulen (VU Amsterdam) [thesis] Hongzhong Yu (VU Amsterdam) [thesis] Lucas de Geus (VU Amsterdam, jointly with Deloitte) [thesis]
